quoteFirst: I looked at my watch by the moonlight, and found that it was
quoteTime: thirteen minutes after two a. m.
quoteLast: Then, thinking I might get a sight of the burglar from our bedroom window, I drew the heavy, old-fashioned curtains aside, and peered out over the silent landscape thirty feet below.
title:
The Adventures of the Eleven Cuff-Buttons
Being one of the exciting episodes in the career of the famous detective Hemlock Holmes, as recorded by his friend Dr. Watson
author: Thierry, James Francis

title: The Chautauquan, Vol. 04, May 1884, No. 8
author: Chautauqua Literary and Scientific Circle; Chautauqua Institution; Flood, Theodore L., 1842- [Editor]

quoteFirst: We knew we could catch a
quoteLast: train at Goslar (a small town about 15 kilometres due north of Clausthal), and after that we had to trust to luck to find trains to take us _via_ Cassel to Rotweil, a village near the Swiss frontier.
title: The Escaping Club
author: Evans, A. J. (Alfred John), 1889-

quoteFirst: During the week Kicq and I changed our plans and determined to go straight by the through train which left Goslar at
quoteLast: to Düsseldorf, and then try to find a Dutch bargee on the Rhine, who could be bribed to take us as far as the frontier and could probably give us information as to the best method of crossing if he could not take us through himself.
title: The Escaping Club
author: Evans, A. J. (Alfred John), 1889-
